EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On the evening of January 20th, a warm front was moving slowly northeast off the Gulf of Mexico with a moist unstable air-mass moving slowly inland across the central Gulf Coast region. A series of slow moving thunderstorms repeatedly moved across the deeply saturated grounds of the western Florida Panhandle, producing numerous reports of flooding and flash flooding across the region. Prior to this event, northwest Florida was experiencing one of it's wettest winter season on record.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Carnley Road near Century, Florida closed due to high water covering the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced large hail in northwest Florida.
Heavy rains caused flooding in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain flooded roads at Mobile Highway and West Fairfield Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains caused flooding in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain flooded roads at West Fairfield Drive and North Pace Boulevard.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains caused flooding in northwest Florida.
Heavy rains caused flooding in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains flooded the road at North Spring Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the area. Strong thunderstorms along and ahead of the boundary allowed for some wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The roof peeled off of a single-wide mobile home at the end of Verges Road in Thonotosassa. No other damage was reported in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A drier than normal wet season along with a very dry October and early November, led to the development of severe drought (D2) conditions over portions of Glades County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rainfall totals over much of Glades County averaged less than 50% of normal for most of the summer and early fall. Since June, rainfall totals were 5 to 7 inches below normal. Some wells in Glades County were running at below 10 percent of normal water levels, with most levels at 10 to 30 percent of normal. The level of Lake Okeechobee was at 13.2 feet in late November, which was about 1.7 feet below normal.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Drier than normal conditions which began in the summer continued through the fall and into December.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rainfall totals continued to be below normal over most of South Florida in December, with most areas receiving only 25 to 50 percent of normal for the month. As a result, severe drought (D2) conditions developed over metro and coastal Palm Beach County by month's end. Rainfall totals since June 1 were as much as 14 inches below normal at West Palm Beach. Most wells across the area were running at about 10 to 30 percent of normal water levels. The level of Lake Okeechobee dropped to 12.4 feet by the end of December, which is 2.2 feet below normal. The Keetch-Byram Drought Index (KBDI) was in the 550 to 600 range, which reflects a high fire danger and very low soil moisture values.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line moved through the area ahead of a cold front. A strong low-level jet in place allowed for some strong gusty winds with the storms and caused some damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large branches were snapped off of several trees along Aquarius Drive in Inverness. Four homes and one mobile home sustained roof damage from the fallen tree branches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Drier than normal conditions which began in the summer continued through the fall and into December.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rainfall totals continued to be below normal over most of South Florida in December, with most areas receiving only 25 to 50 percent of normal for the month. As a result, severe drought conditions (D2) developed over Hendry and western Palm Beach counties by the second week of December. Rainfall totals since June 1 were about 5 to 10 inches below normal in Hendry County as well as western sections of Palm Beach County. Most wells across the area were running at about 10 percent of normal water levels. The level of Lake Okeechobee dropped to 12.4 feet by the end of December, which is 2.2 feet below normal. The Keetch-Byram Drought Index (KBDI) was in the 550 to 600 range, which reflects a high fire danger and very low soil moisture values.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system moved across the area and brought numerous showers and thunderstorms. Lightning from one storm struck a duplex and caused a fire.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck one side of a duplex and caused a fire in the living room area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large swells from a distant storm in the Atlantic Ocean led to large, breaking waves along the southeast Florida coast, most notably along the Palm Beach County coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reports from Ocean Rescue personnel and off-duty NWS employees indicated breaking waves of 10 to 15 feet along the Palm Beach County coast. The last 75 feet of the pier at Juno Beach was closed after seas destroyed 10 to 12 sections of the pier and the railing. Moderate beach erosion was also noted as the surf ran up all the way to the dune line across much of the Palm Beach County coast. ||A small boat flipped in the rough surf near Palm Beach Inlet, but no one was hurt. A woman was also thrown into rocks alongside a jetty, however she was also not hurt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large swells from a distant storm in the Atlantic Ocean led to large, breaking waves along the southeast Florida coast, most notably along the Palm Beach County coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About a dozen people had to be rescued from the surf in Miami Beach as rip currents caused by rough surf and large swells pounded the beaches. A swimmer broke an ankle before being rescued by lifeguards near First Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moderate easterly winds caused the formation of rip currents along the southeast Florida coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tourist drowned and two others were injured in rip currents off 5th street and Ocean Drive. The victims were students of New York University who were taking a last swim before heading back to New York. Miami Beach Ocean Rescue were able to save three of the four swimmers. Time is estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front lifted north through the area and combined with a strong low-level jet and a strong upper-level jet in place over the state. The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down onto a mobile home causing major damage on State Road 54 in Zephyrhills. There was moderate wind damage to another mobile home nearby on Melinda Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front lifted north through the area and combined with a strong low-level jet and a strong upper-level jet in place over the state. The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado touched down between Lake Ariana and Lake Mariana downing numerous power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm moved to the coast from the central Florida interior and briefly and locally reached severe levels as it interacted with a sea breeze boundary along the Brevard County coast. A wind gust to 60 mph was recorded by weather observers at Patrick Air Force Base.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A peak wind gust of 60 mph was recorded at Patrick Air Force Base as a strong thunderstorm briefly and locally became severe before moving offshore. Along Highway A1A just east of the airbase, several hanging traffic signals were damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An isolated thunderstorm developed along the sea breeze boundary during mid afternoon. A storm spotter observed a short-lived funnel cloud in association with the cell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter observed a short-lived funnel cloud as an isolated thunderstorm developed along the sea breeze boundary.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were blown down along Highway 26 and Highway 90.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees and power lines were blown down near Newmans Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down onto a transformer on 600 North Orange Avenue. Trees were blown down onto power lines at 807 Hymon Circle.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were reported blown down near County Road 334A and State Road 26.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A person was trapped in a home when a tree fell onto the structure along Southeast 73rd Terrace.  The cost of damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees, power lines, fences and tree limbs were blown down. There was significant crop damage done. Pea size hail was also observed and 1.62 inches of rainfall fell in about 25 minutes. The cost of crop damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were blown down near the Orange Lake area.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down along Northwest 27th Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were blown down along south U.S. Highway 17.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of convection strengthened across northeast Florida during the afternoon when a 40-50 kt 850mb jet from the southwest phased with the line across a moderately unstable and sheared atmosphere.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree limb was blown down an damaged a car port. The cost of damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed during the afternoon over the central Florida peninsula, east of Orlando. As the storms moved east toward the Atlantic coast, several funnel clouds were spotted and wind damage occurred over east Orange County and over northern Brevard County. Nickel size hail also fell in northern Brevard County. During the early evening, an isolated strong thunderstorm formed along the inland propagating east coast sea breeze boundary and produced nickel sized hail in St. Lucie County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel sized hail was reported during a strong thunderstorm near the intersection of Barton Street and Dover Road in the town of Mims.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed during the afternoon over the central Florida peninsula, east of Orlando. As the storms moved east toward the Atlantic coast, several funnel clouds were spotted and wind damage occurred over east Orange County and over northern Brevard County. Nickel size hail also fell in northern Brevard County. During the early evening, an isolated strong thunderstorm formed along the inland propagating east coast sea breeze boundary and produced nickel sized hail in St. Lucie County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A weather spotter observed nickel size hail during an isolated strong thunderstorm which developed along an inland moving sea breeze boundary. The hail fell near the intersection of Port St. Lucie Boulevard and Darwin Boulevard in Port St. Lucie.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak trough lifted north over the area bringing showers and a few thunderstorms. Moderate to strong onshore winds from the tightened pressure gradient between the trough and high pressure ridging across the region caused rip currents to form along area beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 26-year-old male tourist from Ohio became caught in a rip current off of Siesta Key Beach and drowned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong downburst winds associated with a cluster of slow-moving thunderstorms blew a carport across a road in Palm Bay. Nearby, signs were blown over at a construction site, resulting in one injury.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Police and SKYWARN reports, along with radar data, indicate that a cluster of slow eastward moving thunderstorms produced strong downburst winds which resulted in some minor damage in Palm Bay. An aluminum carport was blown across the road near the intersection of Fears Road SE and San Fillippo Drive. Nearby, signs were blow over at a construction site, resulting in minor injuries to one individual near the intersection of Cogan Drive SE and Toluca Street.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cluster of strong thunderstorms developed east of Orlando and moved toward the coast. As the storms reached the border of Brevard County, they intersected an inland moving sea breeze boundary and briefly intensified to severe levels. Winds blew off a piece of a roof from a home and damaged a fence on Pine Street in Canaveral Groves. Nearby, a power pole was blown down onto a tree, downing powerlines toward the ground. The damage was reported near the intersection of Scarlet Avenue and Pine Street.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm downed many large tree limbs in the Tosohatchee Wildlife Management Area in Orange County south of Highway 50 near the town of Christmas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front lifted north through the area and combined with a strong low-level jet and a strong upper-level jet in place over the state. The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were 28 building damaged along the path of the tornado, eight of which were multifamily condominiums, and five buildings were uninhabitable. Most of the damage was due to uplift of roof decks, partial loss of roofs, failure of porches, and numerous trees ripped apart. One building had the entire roof and an exterior wall ripped off. The roof was held on with nails and not hurricane straps.

The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck an umbrella and shocked the teenager that was holding it. He was hospitalized with minor injuries.

The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public reported nickel sized hail west of Kathleen.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line ahead of a cold front moved rapidly east through South Florida during the late afternoon and early evening of February 12th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at home weather station near Flagler Street and 32nd Avenue in Miami.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported by CoCoRaHS observer in Greenacres City.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail size varied from pea to nickel size.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency manager reported golf ball sized hail at Okeheelee Park near the Florida Turnpike and Forest Hill Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Also reported 40 mph winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed within a marginally unstable air mass well ahead of a cold front. The strongest storms organized along the leading edge of a southward dropping large scale boundary, which moved from the northern to southern Florida peninsula from late morning through early evening. Strong winds aloft translated to surface wind gusts of 40 to 55 mph within several east central Florida Counties. Funnel clouds were observed in Volusia, Seminole and Brevard Counties. Hail of dime to penny size was reported in Orange and Martin Counties and likely occurred across rural areas of other counties as well. Heavy rainfall, totaling 3 to 5 inches produced minor roadway and urban in many locations. No damage was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A NWS Contract observer at the Daytona Beach Airport witnessed multiple funnel clouds form and dissipate, approximately two miles southwest of his location.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms produced gusty winds that caused damage in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts estimated at 70 mph downed trees and along Red Fox Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms produced gusty winds that caused damage in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts estimated at 70 mph blew a small shed into a home. Trees and fences were downed along Iron Gate Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A broken line of thunderstorms associated with a pre-frontal squall line crossed central Florida during the afternoon. While radar indicated numerous storms exhibited rotation aloft, and many areas experienced strong wind gusts which downed small branches, only one storm produced very localized wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds from a severe thunderstorm blew across Lake Washington in Melbourne and lifted the roof off a boat house on the east side of the lake. The heavy wooden roof of the structure landed on the ground about 150 yards away.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms produced gusty winds that caused damage in northwest Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts estimated at 70 mph knocked a chimney over. Small metal structures were also damaged. Trees and power lines were downed along Antelope Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Front door blown in, downed tree branches and flipped vending machine at Lake Worth Road and US 441. Dime size hail also reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm moved through Canal Point, producing strong wind gusts which damaged a carport and moved a pick-up truck about 10 feet. An awning was also torn off a building.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous strong thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front over portions of south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At Palm Beach International Airport. Hail of unknown size also reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving squall line moved across the Florida Keys as a deepening low pressure system moved from the southeastern Gulf of Mexico to the western North Atlantic east of the Florida Peninsula.  An isolated severe thunderstorm wind gust was observed on Long Key.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 50 knots (58 mph) was measured at a Citizen Weather Observer Station at the Florida Keys Aqueduct Authority Pumping Station on Long Key.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the area on February 24th, with cold Canadian high pressure settling over the area behind the front. The night of the 25th into the morning of the 26th was the coldest, with several counties across the northern half of the area experiencing 8 hour or longer sub-freezing durations. With the extreme freezes and losses that occurred in January, this freeze at the end of February caused additional losses to already weakened and newly planted crops.||Estimates of crop damage are listed below by county for the month of February. EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highlands County felt sub-freezing temperatures for 3 to 6 hours across much of the county. The Archbold cooperative station experienced the coldest temperature across the county of 24 degrees. The county has 83,516 acres of harvested farmland, which is approximately $1.32 million in crop damages.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line moved rapidly east across central Florida and produced several strong thunderstorms. Small hail and isolated wind damage occurred with some of the storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An Indian River County Emergency Manager reported nickel sized hail and estimated wind gusts up to around 50 mph as a strong storm moved across Sebastian.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line moved rapidly east across central Florida and produced several strong thunderstorms. Small hail and isolated wind damage occurred with some of the storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An apparent microburst associated with a fast moving strong thunderstorm produced localized wind damage to the Palm Bay Chamber of Commerce building and surrounding area. The roof of a building lost a small portion of its roof, soffits were pulled from another building and a free standing sign was ripped in half. One large tree was toppled and several small palm trees were knocked over.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system centered over the Gulf of Mexico moved northeast across the central Florida peninsula bringing showers and thunderstorms across the area containing strong wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One building sustained shingle and roof damage along 30th Avenue East in Bradenton and tree limbs caused damage to power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms ahead of a cold front developed in the warm and moist pre-frontal enviroment.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ten large trees were blown down in Morriston.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the area bringing strong thunderstorms along and ahead of the boundary. One thunderstorm produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An awning was torn off of a mobile home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several strong thunderstorms moved from south of Orlando to the coast of Brevard County. One of the storms became severe and produced a very weak tornado starting just southwest of Interstate 95 and continuing for 2.4 miles, affecting a small portion of Melbourne. Damage along the track ranged from downed tree branches and palm fronds, to a toppled large tree and minor damage to two structures (one permanent home and one unoccupied mobile home). Winds were estimated at 50 to 60 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very weak EF0 tornado traveled from just southwest of Interstate 95 and Eau Gallie Boulevard In Melbourne to just southwest of the intersection of Aurora Road and Wickham Road. Damage consisted of broken tree branches and palm fronds, to a toppled large tree and minor damage to two structures (one unoccupied mobile home and a permanent home). DI3 DOD1 (LB-EXP).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line ahead of a cold front moved rapidly east through South Florida during the late afternoon and early evening of February 12th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured at City of Miami Beach Fire Headquarters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line ahead of a cold front moved rapidly east through South Florida during the late afternoon and early evening of February 12th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds affected the city of Hialeah. A light post was knocked down along with a few small trees in the southern section of the city near Palm Avenue and Okeechobee Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line moved through the area under the left exit region of a 150 kt upper level jet. The dynamical forcing combined with marginal instability over central Florida where a Slight Risk of Severe Storms existed per the Storm Prediction Center.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A horse farm reported that trees were blown down in the area and a heavy grade horse jumping fence was destroyed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Rain developed north of a surface front as it was lifting northward over north Florida in response to an upper level low digging over eastern Texas. Strong isentropic lift was noted at and above 850 mb with a shallow northeast flow at the surface. The combination of coastal convergence and the lifting front as well as adequate moisture and upper level divergence produced pockets of heavy rainfall across northeast Florida that evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An NWS employee measured 3.51 inches of rainfall in 2 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds within a cluster of pre-frontal showers and thunderstorms downed a section of a large oak tree in Doctors Inlet. The section fell through a home and caused significant structural damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds blew down a section of a large oak tree tree. The section fell through the roof of a home and caused significant damage. The cost of damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dry conditions assisted the growth of a brush fire in southwest Duval county, named the Normandy fire. Units from the Jacksonville Fire and Rescue Department were initially called to the intersection of Normandy Boulevard and Duval County Road 217 at 4:30pm for a report of a brush fire. Upon arrival, fire units reported that they had several small spot fires merging into larger fire. They immediately requested additional units. The fire was brought under control around 9:30 pm that evening with forestry announcing the fire was 50% contained and no homes were threatened. The cause of the fire is under investigation, although it is believed a truck blew a tire and the axle scraped along the road, creating the small spot fires that led to the larger fire.  On the 16h, about 2,000 acres were burned. By December 22nd, the fire was 95% contained an burned about 2600 acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: On the 16h, about 2,000 acres were burned. By December 22nd, the fire was 95% contained an burned about 2600 acres.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wildfire stared in the Lochloosa Wildlife Conservation Area on December 21st and burned an area north of Alachua County Road 325 and west of U.S. Highway 301 near Island Grove. Dry conditions and shifting winds caused the fire to propagate through the end of the month, and flames threatened homes near Cross Creek. On Dec. 24th,the fire had burned 2,740 acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wildfire consumed at least 2,740 acres in the Lochloosa Wildlife Conservation Area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure in place at the surface and aloft kept a hot and moderately moist atmosphere over the area. Scattered afternoon thunderstorms developed along sea breeze and outflow boundaries, with some storms producing large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter-sized hail was reported west of Lutz.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure in place at the surface and aloft kept a hot and moderately moist atmosphere over the area. Scattered afternoon thunderstorms developed along sea breeze and outflow boundaries, with some storms producing large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel-sized hail was reported near Gibsonia along with wind gusts estimated at 50mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure ridging across the area allowed for afternoon sea breeze circulations to develop each day. The warm and moist atmosphere caused scattered thunderstorms containing damaging winds to form along the sea breeze and outflow boundaries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A roof was damaged by a lightning strike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure ridging across the area allowed for afternoon sea breeze circulations to develop each day. The warm and moist atmosphere caused scattered thunderstorms containing damaging winds to form along the sea breeze and outflow boundaries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A privacy fence was blown down by strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist and unstable southwest flow was established at the surface and aloft. In addition, a series of upper disturbances were moving over the area. When combined with daytime heating and sea breeze boundaries, this allowed for numerous strong to severe thunderstorms to develop through the day and continue into the evening, with damaging winds, hail, and a waterspout occurring with the stronger storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public sent a photo to a local news station of quarter-sized hail that fell at their location in Winter Haven.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist and unstable southwest flow was established at the surface and aloft. In addition, a series of upper disturbances were moving over the area. When combined with daytime heating and sea breeze boundaries, this allowed for numerous strong to severe thunderstorms to develop through the day and continue into the evening, with damaging winds, hail, and a waterspout occurring with the stronger storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny-sized hail was reported near the intersection of State Road 54 and Morris Bridge Road near Zephyrhills.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The severe drought conditions which developed in October in Calhoun, Bay, Washington, and southern Walton counties came to an end on November 16th as rainfall increased.
The severe drought conditions which developed in October in Calhoun, Bay, Washington, and southern Walton counties came to an end on November 16th as rainfall increased.
Severe drought conditions (D2) developed in Madison county at the end of the month and continued into December.
An active upper level disturbance moving across Florida triggered severe afternoon thunderstorms over the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotter reported quarter-sized hail near North Kendall Drive and the Florida Turnpike.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active upper level disturbance moving across Florida triggered severe afternoon thunderstorms over the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off-duty NWS meteorologist reported hail slightly larger than a quarter at SW 74th Street and 154th Avenue in West Kendall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active upper level disturbance moving across Florida triggered severe afternoon thunderstorms over the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Public estimated quarter-size to slightly larger hail at SW 117th Avenue and Sunset Drive in Kendall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Atmospheric instability and a westerly wind flow produced severe thunderstorms and flooding in Miami Beach.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home in the Doral Isles neighborhood, causing a large fire in the roof. No one inside the home was injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure ridging across the area allowed for afternoon sea breeze circulations to develop each day. The warm and moist atmosphere caused scattered thunderstorms containing damaging winds to form along the sea breeze and outflow boundaries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The AWOS at MacDill Air Force Base measured a wind gust of 61 knots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure ridging north of the area allowed for scattered late afternoon and evening thunderstorms to develop along seabreeze and outflow boundaries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter sized hail was reported near U.S. Highway 41 near Land O' Lakes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure in place at the surface and aloft kept a hot and moderately moist atmosphere over the area. Scattered afternoon thunderstorms developed along sea breeze and outflow boundaries, with some storms producing large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started a brush fire in the Brooker Creek Preserve, burning 60 acres before being extinguished around 9:30PM. Hikers were immediately evacuated and the trails were closed through Friday morning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moderate southwesterly winds were in place across the area. This caused rip currents to develop along some area beaches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 14-year-old male was caught in a rip current off of Siesta Key Beach Access 10 and nearly drowned. The boy was unconscious when a female beachgoer rescued him, but he was resuscitated and transported to the hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Arson caused a string of 8 to 12 fires over a 6 week period in the Treaty Park area of St. Johns county. Dry conditions fueled fire development.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A string of 8 to 12 wildfires in St. Johns county was due to arson. Fires burned near the Treaty Park area and Flagler Estates. Homes were threatened at times, but no damage was reported. By the afternoon of the 16th, the fires had burned about 12-15 acres.

A surface low tracked eastward from the Gulf of Mexico with a surface warm front extending toward the southern Florida peninsula. Cold air was in place across the area as well as deep moisture. As an upper level short wave trough approached the area from the west, reports of sleet began across portions of north and central Florida after midnight and continued through the late morning hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sporadic reports of sleet began around 11 pm on February 11, 2010, when a trained spotter reported light rain mixed with sleet near Archer. Around 11 am on the 12th, the emergency manager reported light sleet mixed with rain in High Springs. There was no accumulation of sleet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line moved through the area under the left exit region of a 150 kt upper level jet. The dynamical forcing combined with marginal instability over central Florida where a Slight Risk of Severe Storms existed per the Storm Prediction Center.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cold air funnels were reported along the U.S. 301 corridor north of Ocala near Sparr.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered afternoon thunderstorms developed along sea breeze boundaries in a hot and moderately moist environment.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a rotted 45-foot tall oak tree causing it to fall onto a manufactured home in Lake Panasoffkee. The two residents of the home were inside their living room when the tree fell onto them, killing a 59-year-old female and critically injuring a 58-year-old male.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The east coast and west coast sea breezes interacted with outflows from storms over southeast Georgia. These boundaries merged across the Suwannee River Valley of north Florida during the evening hours as a short wave trough was passing over the region in the mid levels. This feature increased vertical instability, and surface based instability was high was CAPEs over 2500 J/kg. A few severe storms erupted from Lake City to Gainesville which produced hail and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail was reported along State Road 24 near the Gainesville Regional Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moderate to strong easterly winds led to the formation of strong rip currents along the Atlantic beaches of South Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 44-year-old man died trying to save a woman who was caught in a rip current about 300 yards north of the Dania Beach Pier. The victim along with 4 other people jumped in the water to save the woman, who according to police, was drunk. ||Winds were east at 15 to 20 mph, ideal conditions for rip currents to form. ||No lifeguards were on duty at the time of the incident.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist and unstable southwest flow was established at the surface and aloft. In addition, a series of upper disturbances were moving over the area. When combined with daytime heating and sea breeze boundaries, this allowed for numerous strong to severe thunderstorms to develop through the day and continue into the evening, with damaging winds, hail, and a waterspout occurring with the stronger storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel-sized hail was reported near Lake Pierce.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced lightning that caused minor injuries during a training exercise on Eglin Reservation.

Southerly flow around high pressure, increasing atmospheric moisture, and a passing mid level short wave trough phased across our western Florida zones during the evening to yield a few severe storms that produced widespread wind damage and some large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down on a home at NW 22nd Avenue with no injuries reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed within a marginally unstable air mass well ahead of a cold front. The strongest storms organized along the leading edge of a southward dropping large scale boundary, which moved from the northern to southern Florida peninsula from late morning through early evening. Strong winds aloft translated to surface wind gusts of 40 to 55 mph within several east central Florida Counties. Funnel clouds were observed in Volusia, Seminole and Brevard Counties. Hail of dime to penny size was reported in Orange and Martin Counties and likely occurred across rural areas of other counties as well. Heavy rainfall, totaling 3 to 5 inches produced minor roadway and urban in many locations. No damage was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An Orlando TV Station weather spotter observed a brief funnel cloud near the intersection of Interstate 4 and State Road 434 in Altamonte Springs.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall impacted Flagler county, specifically areas near Bunnell. Around 1:30 pm on March 11th, county emergency management reported 3.30 inches of rain collected at the Emergency Operations Center.  Most of this rainfall occurred between 11 am and 1:30 pm. At the same time, emergency management relayed a report of 2.9 inches of rainfall in East Bunnell since 11 am that morning. By 10 am of March 12th, emergency management measured a 24-hour storm total rainfall amount of 5.01 inches at Haw Creek, which is about 8 miles west southwest of Bunnell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure tracked north of the area while strong sub-tropical jet stream dynamics continued across Florida. Areas of moderate to heavy rainfall and scattered thunderstorms impacted areas mainly south of Interstate 10 across north Florida during the evening hours and around midnight. Wind gusts of 44 to 46 mph were recorded during the evening across Northeast Florida around 10 am.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds blew down a tree that damaged two cars and knocked over a light post. The cost of damage was estimated at $4,000. At 11 pm, the Gainesville Regional Airport measured sustained winds of 21 mph and a gust to 40 mph.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two homes on Stone Road incurred damage. The first was an older home that had a small portion of the roof torn off. The second home had shingles torn off the roof and damage to the pool screen.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The pool cages on two homes on Bayway Court in the Gulf Landings Subdivision sustained damage.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Police and fire officials reported a large tree toppled by high winds as a severe thunderstorm moved across the region. The tree penetrated through portions of a home. The home was heavily damaged and was declared condemned.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains flooded numerous roadways around the Crestview area.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency manager reported 1 inch of snow in Jay.

Later around 2:30 pm, a spotter near 103rd Street and 84th Avenue southwest of Ocala measured 6 inches of rainfall between 8 am and 1 pm. No property damage estimate was available, but a default of $1,000 was entered which may account for road repair in the wake of the flood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front lifted north through the area and combined with a strong low-level jet and a strong upper-level jet in place over the state. The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were several road closures across Holiday, Bayonet Point, Dade City, and Zephyrhills due to high waters and flooded streets. Throughout the entire event, the Cooperative Station at Saint Leo received 4.10 inches of rain. A mesonet site about one mile east-northeast of Zephyrhills had 4.75 inches of rain. Other mesonet sites had 4.37 inches 2 miles east-northeast of New Port Richey, 4.33 inches 1.8 miles west-southwest of Dade City, and 4.02 inches 2 miles north-northeast of Port Richey.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A mesonet station 2 miles northeast of Lutz reported 5.57 inches of rain throughout the event causing minor street flooding. In addition, another mesonet station 4 miles west-northwest of Lutz reported 5.52 inches of rain. The gauge at the Hillsborough River State Park near Thonotosassa reported 3.28 inches of rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong shortwave trough moved across the forecast area as a surface low tracked across northeast Florida from the Gulf of Mexico. Heavy rainfall was produced across much of northeast Florida with storm total rainfall amounts of 3 to 6 inches. A few strong storms occurred across Marion County where surface based instability was highest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail was reported in Ocala.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front lifted north through the area and combined with a strong low-level jet and a strong upper-level jet in place over the state. The warm and moist environment in place allowed for numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop. A few tornadoes touched down and some areas received heavy rains causing flooding from these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A C-MAN station in Venice reported 4.55 inches of rain throughout the event causing minor flooding. In addition, other mesonet stations received 4.32 inches of rain 1 mile southwest of Venice and 4.08 inches about 2 miles north-northwest of Englewood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Saturated grounds from previous rains, along with recent heavy rain and 30 mph wind gusts from a thunderstorm passing 30 minutes earlier, caused a large pine tree to topple as a vehicle was traveling beneath, resulting in a fatality.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Weather likely played an indirect role in a vehicle-related fatality in DeLand. Saturated ground existed across the area due to several previous days of localized heavy rainfall. Between 1400 and 1500 LST, a thunderstorm moved southwest across DeLand, resulting in a quick two inches of additional rain and wind gusts to around 30 mph. These conditions combined with the pre-existing saturated ground, likely weakened and helped topple a large pine tree. A moving pickup truck was hit by the falling tree, then ran off the shoulder of the road as the driver lost control.  The woman passenger was killed and the driver, her husband, was critically injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak trough of low pressure and an unstable atmosphere resulted in an isolated severe storm over Madison County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees and power lines down from Madison to just south of Lee on Highway 255. One tree fell on a house in Madison.  The monetary damage figure provided is a rough estimate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cluster of thunderstorms moved across the Kennedy Space Center (KSC) after interaction with the east coast sea breeze farther to the west. A brief funnel cloud formed in association with a thunderstorm near the coast north of Cape Canaveral. The funnel was observed by a weather observer at the Shuttle Landing Facility and was also observed and photographed by several KSC employees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Observers at the Shuttle Landing Facility saw a brief funnel cloud develop just off to the northeast.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was over the area aloft with the surface Bermuda ridge axis south of the area. A light and mean southwest steering flow was over the area with an unstable low level air mass in place. Storms formed along the sea breezes and propagated inland through the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large magnolia tree was blown down in Murray Hill.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Weak steering flow was in place with a very moist air mass over the area. A trough was to the north and a ridge south of the area with weak vorticity advection over the area. A cluster of storms moved northwest from Marion county across Alachua county. Periods of very heavy rainfall and wind gusts around 30 mph caused some storm damage in Gainesville.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Gainesville Regional Airport measured a wind gust of 22 knots with an outflow boundary that moved northward from a storm complex that was dissipating over Marion County. One tree was blown down and blocked one lane of traffic on Schoolhouse Road in southwest Gainesville The time of the event was based on radar, and the cost of damage was estimated.

Numerous storms fired across the area during the afternoon and evening as the sea breezes and their outflows converged between Highway 301 and Interstate 95.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter measured 1.42 inches of rainfall in 45 minutes. A 42 mph wind gust was also measured along with small limbs down.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain produced flash flooding over many areas across northwest FL and the adjacent panhandle.  Wakulla county reported 6-7 inches of rain during the mid to late morning, with 3-6 inches across most of the region beginning late Wednesday night (Jan. 20th). There were widespread reports of flooded roadways, with water in some homes in Wakulla county.  FSU campus in Tallahassee reported 50-60 cars flooded.  Two hikers were stranded and clinging to trees along the Florida Trail near Freeport in Walton county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough was over the area with a surface trough located north of the Altamaha River basin. This energy combined with a moist and unstable air mass enabled scattered severe storms to form over the area along and in advance of the sea breezes and resultant outflows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread street flooding was reported in downtown Jacksonville and Hogan's Creek overflowed its banks. Water was as deep as 2 feet at the intersection of 1st Street and Pearl Street. Many streets had to be closed for several hours in the evening throughout downtown including Independent Drive near the Modis Building; the intersection of Newman and State Streets; the 1000 Block of North Laura Street; the intersection of East 9th and Walnut Streets and the intersection of West 12th and Davis Streets.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter reported a funnel cloud over the St. Johns River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The collision of the East Coast and Gulf Coast sea breeze boundaries over the central Florida interior resulted in several storms which produced damaging winds and penny sized hail from Oviedo to Orlando to St. Cloud. Trees fell onto two mobile homes and produced minor damage. A young boy in one of the mobile homes sustained minor injuries to his arm as a tree crashed onto the home.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sea breeze boundaries collided and produced a severe thunderstorm which damaged several mobile homes within the Colonial Village Mobile Home Park in Orlando. The winds downed several trees, damaged siding and gutters on several mobile homes and destroyed a car port.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two separate funnel clouds developed during the morning along isolated, but heavy sea-breeze induced showers. The first funnel cloud was sighted near Ft. Pierce and the second one occurred over Port Canaveral. Each funnel cloud was witnessed for 15-20 minutes and was reported by numerous individuals.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two St. Lucie County Sheriff Deputies observed a stationary funnel cloud near the intersection of Kings Highway and Indrio Road in Ft. Pierce.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced sporadic large hail and damaging winds, along with more widespread flooding through the morning.

A weakening surface front was near southeast Georgia with a surface ridge axis across central Florida. A mean 1000-700 mb ridge axis was over southeast Georgia with a light south to southwest steering flow over the local area. An unstable low level air mass was in place and combined with the sea breezes and outflows numerous severe storms developed across the area during the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A back porch was blown off of a house and landed in the neighbors yard at 7219 Southeast Second Place.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A continuation of strong east winds caused by seasonal high pressure over the western Atlantic created strong rip currents along the SE Florida coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rip currents nearly drowned a teenager swimming at a guarded beach in Boca Raton. Lifeguards rescued the teen and performed CPR to revive him. The boy made a full recovery.
